page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.4xlarge is a burstable instance type built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a frequency of 2.5 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core, paired with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is relatively weak, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 25% and bottom 10% respectively. Single-threaded tasks like gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show poor performance. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, PassMark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core, reach average tiers. Memory bandwidth tests for both small and large blocks consistently register in the average range, though the PassMark memory score is weak.

For workload execution, the instance performs adequately in multi-threaded operations such as software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving, all of which score in the average range. It also handles Redis SET operations and small-model LLM inference with average throughput, though performance drops to weak when processing larger 7B parameter models. Given its burstable CPU allocation and lack of local storage, this instance is qualitatively cost-effective for workloads with variable CPU demands, such as development environments, code compilation, and low-to-medium traffic web serving, where continuous peak CPU utilization is not required.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.4xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 16 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 2.5 GHz. Built on the KVM hypervisor, this server does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps. Benchmark data shows weak single-core performance, with single-threaded compression and text processing scoring in the bottom decile. Conversely, multi-core operations, including software compilation, ray tracing, and static web serving, achieve average performance tiers. The burstable CPU allocation makes this instance a qualitatively cost-effective option for workloads with variable resource demands. It is best suited for development environments, multi-threaded build jobs, and general-purpose web serving that do not require sustained peak CPU performance.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.4xlarge is a burstable KVM instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8269CY CPU, delivering average multi-core performance for variable workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-c1m2.4xlarge is a burstable KVM instance with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and average multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A burstable multi-core instance with balanced memory and baseline networking for variable, non-sustained compute workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8269CY processor with 16 vCPUs and 32.0 GB RAM
Burstable CPU allocation suitable for variable workload demands
Average performance in multi-core benchmarks and software compilation
Weak single-core and single-threaded processing capabilities
No local storage and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Well-suited for development environments and light web serving
